paula.bio:
Г°ВџВ‡В©Г°ВџВ‡ВҒ Ich bereite dich auf die B1-PrГѓВәfung vor.
Г°ВџВҺВҮAlles fГѓВәr B1 - einfach erklГѓВ¤rt!
Г°ВџВЏВ† 19 Jahre Erfahrung |15000+ Teilnehmende
Г°ВџВ”ВӨ Grammatik-CrashcourseГ°ВџВ‘В‡
www.language-progress.com/crashcourse
paula.authority: The subject doesn't show herself as a teacher, she more so blends with the topic itself by getting into characters and using no extended introductions to videos. She rather presents the language itself.
paula.quality: The German is mostly correct but phonetically flawed. She is still above-average compared to other teachers and German speakers I heard speak in Poland. Considering her posting frequency however and briefness of the content itself I think that it's more of a promo tool than tangible help for learners.
paula.psr: Her acting is subliminally quirky. The vulnerable struggles played can be very relatable for viewers. She sits in a hotel room in a bathrobe in the sink reel which can seem very intimate.
paula.selling: It is seperated. Not even her reels' descriptions point at her product. She hopes for sales through her profile instead of the content pieces directly.
paula.freeform: I have to say that she does make a very competent impression and I would even say she, to a certain extent, does a pretty solid job. But the task that she has given herself is very easy and I don't see a big work ethic behind it. Not to call her lazy. The videos themselves sometimes show production quality because she even stood at a real receptionist desk in a fitting outfit. Her accent is probably only noticeable to real native speakers, because the stress placements and some endings or phoneme connections are what exposes her. If I were a German learner I would have the impression that she is perfect.
paula.r1_notes: She starts the reel simply by saying "A1 kontra B1". It's okay she wants to jump right in, however for me as a native speaker the term "kontra" sounds odd. I personally would have used the term 'gegen' or even 'versus' despite the fact that it's an anglicism, even though I would understand caution with non-german vocab, I merely want to note it'd be at least natural sounding. The following terminology she presents e.g. fragen -> sich erkundigen is useful and a good idea, but the terms she compares are often only associated but misleading to be taken as exact replacer. For example she presents B1 'GebГѓВ¤ude' as upgrade to A1 'Haus', but one needs to understand the broader context and it makes it all a bit of a slippery slope in my perception. Her pronunciation is quite okay, but flawed, and she wrongly pronounces the -ig ending in southern German style which I find problematic because she should teach Hochdeutsch.
paula.r2_notes: I like the idea and I have to say even though she is not sovereign as an actress (too much hesitation in character when ordering food) it is a helpful reel to learners. I have to note though that she said "Ich hГѓВ¤tt' gerne" which is very odd, because a native speaker would either articulate the entire sentence cleanly or if elliptic they would say "Ich hГѓВ¤tte gern", but not the variation she presented - it may exist but I feel like it is way less common.
paula.r3_notes: The reel is super short and contains minimal teaching, it's simply "ich werde trinken", "ich trinke", "ich habe getrunken" which gives her little room for error. Her pronunciation is semi-alright again, meaning decent but with accent.
paula.r4_notes: She presents the words beiГѓВџen, kauen, schmatzen, ausspucken. I don't like her pronunciation of schmatzen, she widens the "a" and shortens the "tz". She stresses it /'schma,tzn/.
paula.r5_notes:
The reel itself is quite good, criticizing this one would be harsh. Above her usual performance. In this one however the description is flawed. 

"Wie bitte um die Rechnung? Г°ВџВ’ВіГ°ВџВ‡В©Г°ВџВ‡ВҒ

In diesem Reel zeige ich dir 3 verschiedene MГѓВ¶glichkeiten [...]"

Incomplete sentence, subject is lacking.
paula.r6_notes:
Pronunciation is alright with occasional stress placement faults.
Vocab presented is correct.
On-screen caption says "schГѓВ¶ne beschreung" - Noun wrongly put in lowercase!
paula.r7_notes: Nothing to correct. Very plain and flat reel that correctly corrects the grammar in 5 sentences.
paula.r8_notes: She plays a character struggling to find the right words for "the sink is clogged". In the end of the reel she plays the receptionist saying "Das Waschbecken ist verstopft". Honestly I find this reel alright and helpful.

ania.bio:
рџ‡©рџ‡Ғ UczД™ dorosЕ‚ych mГіwiД‡ po niemiecku
рџ’­ Potocznie. Naturalnie. Bez chaosu i stresu.
вқЊ Niemiecki nie chce ruszyД‡? OdkrД™cimy to!
в¤№ Zapis na nasze lekcje
linktr.ee/ania_germania
ania.authority: Same as Paula Boinska she doesn't really. She goes straight to the point however in her case she does a little polish opener hook before to tell her viewers what the reel will be about.
ania.quality: Similarly, her content sometimes leans useful but mostly lacks structure. I doubt that a learner would seriously benefit from spending time on her reels.
ania.psr: Eye contact, smile, camera angles look consciously picked for attractiveness, perhaps authority. She uses (probably) fictional stories to tell a narrative (e.g. "I have just been scammed! This is how I'm feeling now!").
ania.freeform: I liked her way more than Paula Boinska because she comes across as less tryhard serious, her smile reads as more genuine and she hesitates way less, she actually pulls through with her sentences in instant fashion, even while still a bit flawed or wrong she owns them with confidence.
ania.r1_notes:
Her accent is also Polish but it's not the least pleasant. However she does this typical uvular trill [КЂ], like many Poles. It's off-putting.
The vocab presented is correct, however I found the prepositions she used odd. I'm not sure if my gut feeling is right, but her presented "Ich sehe schlecht in der Ferne" would sound better to me if it was "Ich sehe schlecht AUS der Ferne".
Her stress-placement leaves room for adjustment, especially since in the beginning she says [Ich bin 'kurzsichtig] but later in the video she says [Ich bin 'weit'sichtig] (double stress). That's definitely not correct to teach.
ania.r2_notes:
This reel was made as a trap song, with an altered voice saying different german sentences like "Ich mache mein Bett", put on a loop (one repetition per sentence). The musical production completely distracts from pronunciation markers, but since I have to judge it I would say these ones are pretty smooth.
The ironic thing is that in this comment section people started correcting her Polish translations as wrong, for example commenting "W co ubierasz piЕәamД™?", implementing wrong Polish grammar even though she is a native speaker of Polish.
ania.r3_notes: The author presents the sentences "Ich kippe gleich um", "Ich muss mal an die Frische Luft" and "Ich muss mal runterkommen". The sentences hit the right tone when it comes to casualness and authenticity and the pronunciation is great, with the lone exception that she formed [luЛђft] instead of the correct [lКЉft].
ania.r4_notes: Collab reel with the creator @deutsch.mit.lucy. She presents different German vocabulary, and Lucy presents the Austrian counterpart for each. Nothing to correct apart from their usual imperfect pronunciations.
ania.r5_notes: The video presents colloquial ways of expressing being busy and having a lot on one's mind. There is one drastic mistake in this video at the very last expression. The author says "Bei mir ist die HГәlle los", thinking she says "Bei mir ist die HГ¶lle los". This wrong vowel formation creates a different noun and misleads the audience.
ania.r6_notes: The author presents 4 ways of expressing anger - namely 'stinksauer' 'genervt', 'wГәtend' and 'angepisst'. Actually she is very on point in this video, the only thing is that I would translate 'genervt' to 'zdenerwowany' instead of her translation choice of 'poirytowany'.
ania.r7_notes: The video presents the expressions "Nicht meine Baustelle", "nicht mein Bier", "nicht mein Zirkus, nicht meine Affen". I only know nicht mein Bier, I have never stumbled across the remaining ones, so I am unable to judge those.
ania.r8_notes: The video starts with the hook "These 3 sentences will evolve your German". It's a gray area. The claim is perhaps undisprovable, but in most cases a massive exaggeration. Conscious or unconscious persuasion strategy suspicion. Also the sentences presented are "mir nichts, dir nichts", ['SpaГџ 'beiseite] and "BlГ¶d gelaufen". Dumm gelaufen is more common so she should have in my gut feeling presented that one instead. And as just noted, the stress placement is too active, she doesn't hit the notes with it. Also in total the expressions are colloquial, authentic and useful but unrelated with one another which makes this reel seem so random, especially considering her opening hook - it makes it sound a little silly and harder to take serious.
